metadata.title: Auto-credit unwind — First Brands/UBS/Jefferies, Tricolor/JPMorgan, Carvana/DriveTime/Garcia, and the subprime auto-loan stress (both sides, dated)
metadata.status: the First Brands + Tricolor 2025 bankruptcies, the lender exposures, Carvana/DriveTime/Garcia structure, and the subprime-auto-delinquency data are FACT (court filings, bank disclosures, news of record); fraud allegations are flagged as ALLEGED/under-investigation, not adjudicated
metadata.frame: Ties the auto thread (#177) into the corpus's financial-contagion core: the 2025 leveraged auto-credit unwind. Two near-simultaneous Sept-2025 failures — First Brands (auto-parts supplier; off-balance-sheet supply-chain finance) and Tricolor (subprime auto lender; alleged double-pledged collateral) — burned bank + private-credit lenders (Jefferies, UBS, JPMorgan, Fifth Third) and revived 'who else is hiding losses' fear (Dimon's 'cockroaches'). Layered on Carvana (used-car retailer + subprime ABS, controlled by the Garcia/DriveTime family) and record subprime auto delinquencies. Extends existing First_Brands, Tricolor_Holdings, Subprime_Auto_ABS, PrivateCredit_Funds, UBS, Jefferies, JPMorgan nodes.

the_first_brands: First Brands Group — a roll-up auto-parts supplier (Raybestos, FRAM, TRICO, Carter) — filed Chapter 11 in Sept 2025 under ~$10B+ of liabilities, much of it OFF-BALANCE-SHEET supply-chain finance / receivables factoring. A court-appointed examiner/lenders flagged that roughly $2.3B of receivables-finance funds could not be accounted for — a structure widely compared to GREENSILL (the supply-chain-finance firm whose 2021 collapse helped sink Credit Suisse, which UBS later absorbed). grade: fact (the filing + the missing-funds question); 'fraud' is alleged/under examination.
the_first_brands_lenders: Exposed lenders: Jefferies (its Leucadia-linked Point Bonita / first-lien receivables funds had large First Brands exposure), UBS (its O'Connor hedge-fund unit held First Brands supply-chain paper — a Greensill echo for the bank that absorbed Credit Suisse), plus a wide web of CLO / private-credit holders of First Brands' ~$6B+ leveraged loans. grade: fact (disclosed exposures).
the_tricolor: Tricolor Holdings — a subprime auto lender to thin-file/often-undocumented borrowers — collapsed into bankruptcy in Sept 2025 amid allegations of FRAUD, including the same auto collateral allegedly PLEDGED to multiple lenders. JPMorgan took a ~$170M charge and Fifth Third disclosed exposure; the back-to-back First Brands + Tricolor failures prompted Jamie Dimon's 'when you see one cockroach, there are probably more' warning about hidden credit losses. grade: fact (bankruptcy + charges); fraud allegations are alleged/under-investigation.
the_carvana: Carvana — the online used-car retailer — runs on subprime auto lending + securitization (it sells loans via forward-flow to Ally and into auto ABS). It is controlled by the GARCIA family: Ernest Garcia III is CEO, and his father Ernest Garcia II (Carvana's largest shareholder, who also owns DriveTime, the subprime dealer/servicer that backstops Carvana's loans) pleaded guilty to bank fraud in the 1990 Lincoln Savings/Keating S&L scandal. Carvana nearly collapsed under debt in 2022, then sharply recovered (2023-25) — leaving loan-quality + related-party (DriveTime) questions. grade: fact (structure + Garcia history + 2022 distress/recovery).
the_macro: The backdrop: US auto-loan balances at record highs (~$1.6T), record SUBPRIME delinquencies (60+ day) in 2024-25, widespread negative equity, and rising repossessions — stressing auto ABS. Both sides: (A) systemic-alarm reading — twin 2025 failures + record delinquencies + opaque private-credit/supply-chain finance = an unwind with hidden 'cockroaches'; (B) contained reading — First Brands/Tricolor are idiosyncratic frauds/blowups, prime auto credit is fine, and losses are absorbable. grade: both documented.
the_honest_reading: The 2025 auto-credit unwind is real and dated: First Brands (supply-chain-finance opacity, ~$2.3B unaccounted, a Greensill echo hitting Jefferies + UBS) and Tricolor (subprime lender, alleged double-pledged collateral, hitting JPMorgan + Fifth Third) failed within weeks, reviving the 'cockroaches' fear about private credit + off-balance-sheet finance — atop Carvana's Garcia/DriveTime related-party structure and record subprime auto delinquencies. Fraud claims are flagged as alleged/under-investigation; the systemic-vs-contained reading is presented both ways. This connects the auto industry to the corpus's contagion/private-credit threads. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.
